titleStmt.title: SII 26.689: original edition by P. R. Srinivasan
No. 689. (A.R. No. 644 of 1909.) KŌNĒRIRĀJAPURAM, THANJAVUR TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.

summary.p: This inscription contains the praśasti of the king commencing with Tirumagaḷ pōla, etc.

It records a gift of 1/4 of a lamp to be burnt in the temple of the god Āditta Īśvaramuḍaiya-mahādēvar of Tirunallam, a Brahmadēyam in Veṇṇāḍu by a certain Veḷḷāḷan named Kāḍaṉ Aṭṭāṉatti of the same place, who had endowed 7 kāśu for the purpose. It is stated that this money was received by three persons of the temple, named Kaviśiyan Nakkaṉ Tarukkaṉ and his sons Tarukkaṉ Nārāyaṇaṉ and Tarukkaṉ Naraśiṅga-baṭṭaṉ, who had agreed to burn the lamp. They had also agreed that in case their rights in the temple was mortgaged or sold, those who came in possession of the rights, should also continue to burn the lamp.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . tirumakaḷ polap perunilac celviyun taṉakke Urimai pūṇṭamai maṉakkoḷak kān
taḷūr cālai kalamaṟuttaruḷi veṅkai nāṭum nuḷampapāṭiyum taṭikaipāṭiyuṅ kuṭamalaināṭun taṇṭāl koṇṭu taṉ
ṉeḻil vaḷar Ūḻiyuḷellā yāṇṭun toḻuteka viḷaṅkum yāṇṭe ceḻiyarait
tecukoḷ ko Irājarājarājakesaripaṉmaṟkku yāṇṭu 10 5 Āvatu veṇṇāṭṭu brahma
teyam tirunallattu śrīkoyiluṭaiya kavuciyaṉ nakkaṉ tarukkaṉum Ivaṉ makaṉ tarukkaṉ nārā
yaṇaṉum Ivaṉ tampi tarukkaṉ naraciṅkapaṭṭaṉum Imuvom Ivūr veḷḷāḷaṉ kāṭaṉ Aṭṭāṉattiṉi
ṭaikkoṇṭa kācu yeḻu Ikkācu yeḻukkum kāl viḷakku Ittirunallamuṭaiya Ātitta Īśvaramuṭai
ya mahādevarkku cantirātittaval kāl viḷakkukku ṉicatam Eṇṇai Oru piṭiyaṭṭi Eṅkaḷ muṟaikaḷile E
rittu maṟṟu muṟai ceyvārkku Aḷantaṭṭic cantirātittaval Erippikka kaṭavomākavum Eṅkaḷ ṉāḷo
ṉpatu Oṉpatiṉāl vanta Arccaṉaiyum Arccanābhogamum Ivaṉaittum vilai koṇṭārum Oṟṟi koṇṭārum śrītanampe
ṟṟārum śantrātittaval Ivviḷakkuk kālum muṭṭāmey Erittu ṉāḷḷonpatum Anubhavippārākavum Ipparicu koṇṭom Imuvom—
Digital edition of SII 26.689 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
